'''Suuda''' was a [[Gender/Legends|male]] [[Hutt/Legends|Hutt]]. Along with [[Moosh]], he was one of four rival Hutt [[Gangster/Legends|gangsters]] to reside on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Sleheyron/Legends|Sleheyron]]<ref name="post" /> in [[3956 BBY]] during the [[Jedi Civil War]].<ref name="date">''[[Knights of the Old Republic Campaign Guide]]''</ref> He was involved in an intra-planetary [[Smuggler/Legends|smuggling]] plot which culminated with<ref name="post" /> the [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] [[Revan/Legends|Revan]]<ref name="KOTOR">''[[Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic]]''</ref> being asked to [[Assassin/Legends|assassinate]] Suuda.<ref name="post" />

{{GameLS}}
After working several jobs involved with the smuggling plot, Revan was asked to [[Death/Legends|kill]] Suuda. Revan gained an audience with Suuda and, instead of attacking the Hutt, revealed the plot and was given a reward.<ref name="post" />
{{Endgame}}

==Behind the scenes==
{{Quote|One of them was named Suuda the Hutt and I liked him a lot, he was very catty.|David Gaider on Suuda|<ref name="post" />}}
Suuda, like the planet on which he resided, was originally planned to be part of the<ref name="post" /> [[2003]]<ref name="KOTOR" /> [[Star Wars video games|video game]] ''[[Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic]]'', but was cut from the final release. This was revealed in a post on the now-defunct ''Knights of the Old Republic'' forums by the game's lead writer, [[David Gaider]]. He responded to a question about an in-game mention of Sleheyron by<ref name="post" /> the [[Twi'lek/Legends|Twi'lek]]<ref name="KOTOR" /> [[Yuthura Ban|Yuthura]], elaborating on the planet, Moosh, Suuda and the planned role they had in the game.<ref name="post" />

Four Hutts, of which only Moosh and Suuda are named, were to give the player's character jobs known as side quests. The assassination which was to conclude Suuda's subplot was planned to have an alternate ending, with the player killing Suuda and completing the assassination. Gaider liked Suuda, feeling the Hutt was "catty". He expressed the opinion that the decision to cut the Sleheyron content was sound, feeling the planet and it's subplots were too complex in comparison to the entirety of the rest of the game.<ref name="post" />
